title: Chapter 23
text: Chapter Twenty B y midday, Lara was tempted to leave. There were at least twenty buildings on this street alone, all of them full of stuff just begging to be picked. Why hadn’t Ronin thought of that ? Because he’s a scavenger, not a thief . He was honest, but not in the way other bots had been. There was a thoughtfulness to Ronin’s honesty. He put real consideration into keeping his word, often going well beyond whatever his side of the bargain was. Providing for Lara, in the vague terms they’d agreed upon, would have meant the bare minimum for other bots . But just because Ronin wouldn’t steal didn’t mean Lara couldn’t . If it weren’t such a stupid thing to do, she’d go out now and poke around the nearby buildings. She didn’t think any of them were inhabited. Who would it hurt if some metal appliances or pieces of plastic disappeared? The only real risk would be if she was spotted by the gearheads . She cast aside her thoughts of sneaking out as Tabitha’s face, eyes dull and glossy, flashed through her imagination. Lara was trapped here until Ronin returned . The day wore on slowly, just as the others had while he was gone. Her fingers were still sore from sewing Ronin’s shirt, so she didn’t so much as look at the remaining cloth, and there wasn’t anything else to do downstairs. So, she went up into the attic and passed the time flipping through the pictures in the old books — even the ones full of naked women . As she looked them over, Lara compared herself to these long-dead women. The similarities were slowly growing as time passed . If she walked into the human settlement now, fully clothed, they’d know just from her face. They’d envy her, spurn her, call her a bot-banger and in the same breath lust after her . What did Ronin see when he looked at her ? She smiled, recalling the way he’d touched her — like he couldn’t stop, couldn’t get enough. He seemed fascinated by her skin. What did he feel when they came into contact? Did he compare her body to those of the too-perfect female synths ? It didn’t really matter, in the end. He’d chosen Lara, had married her . Setting Ronin’s rifle aside, Lara rose onto her knees and gazed out the attic window, searching the road for Ronin’s approaching figure. Wishful thinking ; he’d only left last night, and said he’d be back in a few days. This time, she doubted he’d return early. He was serious about earning enough for them to leave without having to look back . He’d only just returned, they’d only just deepened their relationship, and he was gone again. But wasn’t it her fault? She’d been the one to suggest they leave home. She’d begged to go into the Dust with him, anywhere but — Home ? When had she started thinking of this place as home? It was a prison, a deathtrap, a locked cage within a camp full of enemies. Given that, she still saw it as home. As their home. She couldn’t step out the front door without risking her life, but in here, with Ronin, things were … good . She sat, leaning against a stack of boxes, and tilted her head back. She closed her eyes . Ronin was the key. He treated her with considerable patience and kindness, regardless of how deserving of it she was at any given moment. And, damn it, she missed him . She loved him . There it was. Lara didn’t know how it had happened, but she could admit it to herself freely now. In the short time she’d known him, she’d fallen in love with Ronin. A bot. What would the other humans think? She dwelled on the thought for a time, absently running her thumb along the smooth underside of the ring . She didn’t give a shit about what they’d say. Ronin was alive, and Lara loved him . The warm sunshine on her face cast a reddish glow through her eyelids. She opened her eyes; the sun was setting, spreading muted pinks and oranges through the haze beyond the park. One day down, two or three more to go. Once he got back, they’d sell all the scrap and go, and she’d never have to worry about Warlord again. She wasn’t so naïve as to think there’d be no other danger, but she couldn’t imagine anything worse than dealing with him again . She picked up the rifle and descended the ladder . By the time she ate and showered, it was full dark outside. Lara double-checked the locks — even the minor security they provided was better than nothing — and went up to Ronin’s room. She slipped beneath the blankets he only used when he laid with her, laid the rifle beside her, and rested her head on his pillow . Lara inhaled deeply; it was faint, but his earthy scent lingered . Nights were the worst when she was waiting for Ronin. After Tabitha left, Lara had spent her nights alone, and slept soundly despite her concerns. Why was it so difficult now? Every noise the house made, every subtle creak and groan, had her hand tightening around the rifle . The answer was simple. Ronin made her feel safe, not this place. She trusted him enough to let her guard down. Even before they shared a bed, she’d been aware of his presence, and knew he’d come at the first sign of trouble. Just like her first night here, when she nearly broke her ass on the cold bathroom floor . The sooner she went to sleep, the sooner tomorrow would come, and she’d be that much closer to his return. Breathing deeply, she relaxed, imaging his body pressed against hers . Just as she was drifting off, a thump startled her awake. She lay there, staring at the ceiling, and strained to hear more. A bang downstairs had her scrambling from beneath the covers. She snatched up the rifle and crept to the wall, chest heaving and heart pounding . Another thump , followed by a scrape, like something was dragged across the floor. Swallowing hard, she adjusted her grip on the gun, holding it how Ronin showed her before he left. She wasn’t going to hide and wait for whatever that was to catch her. Lara would fight. If she was going to die tonight, it wouldn’t be cowering in the dark . She slipped through the open door and into the hallway, walking silently across the soft carpet. Pressing herself to the wall beside the stairs, she took another stabilizing breath, and waited . There was more noise below. A heavy step, and then that dragging sound. It repeated a few times, ending with the groaning wood of the chair at Ronin’s worktable. Whatever the thing was, it was sitting in Ronin’s chair . Lara peered around the corner. It was dark in the main room; she’d kept the curtains drawn while he was away, for fear of being seen. Everything was silent now, except her own heartbeat . Jumping out a second-floor window would mean a broken leg, most likely, which was as good as death, and getting into the attic now would be too noisy. There were other places to hide, but if it started looking…why the hell was it just sitting down there ? Fuck . Squeezing her eyes shut, she leaned her head back against the wall. I can do this. Ronin said this thing packs a punch, even against bots . If she could at least hit the thing, she might have a chance to run out the front door and hide somewhere else until Ronin came back. That meant she’d have to see it. Once she flipped the switch, she’d only have a second to aim and fire. So long as it hadn’t been moved, she knew exactly where the chair was, knew where to point the gun … Every step down the stairs was agonizing, her lungs burning with the exertion of keeping her breath silent. The carpet padded her footfalls, but she settled her weight carefully, knowing the softest creak would give her away . She focused her eyes on the shadows where the chair would be, darting her gaze to the side only to spot the barely-visible white of the light switch on the nearby wall . I can do this . Lara flicked the light on and leapt to the bottom of the stairs. She swung the rifle up, planted the butt against her shoulder, and pointed the barrel toward the figure in the chair. She squeezed the trigger . The sound was a boom of thunder, filling up the entire room, and the unexpected kick of the gun nearly threw her off-balance. She didn’t waste any time; she turned and ran toward the front door . Something thudded and scraped behind her. A hand closed around her ankle, abruptly halting her movement. The rifle slipped from her hands as she fell, clattering to the floor a few feet away. Screaming, she clawed at the floorboards and kicked wildly at the thing behind her, scrabbling for the gun. The thing caught her other ankle and dragged her back. Her oversized shirt bunched around her waist, baring her . The world spun as it flipped Lara onto her back. The bot loomed over her, pressing a hand to her chest to hold her down . Its clothing was blackened and tattered. Scorched, melted skin clung to its exposed metal. It smelled faintly like burning rubber. The metal face shifted into something that might have been a human expression, if it had skin, but its teeth were locked in a skeleton grin behind the small plates that would have moved its lips. The only bots she’d seen with exposed metal like this were the gearheads . Her fear escalated into terror. If it meant to kill her, that was one thing; it’d be done quick. But the way it was suspended over her, the way it held her pinned down … “Lara,” it said . Warlord’s face flashed in her mind’s eye. She thrashed beneath the inhuman force holding her captive, kicked and slapped, ignoring her pain as she struck its solid body . “Lara,” the bot said again, its voice penetrating her terror . She stilled. The bot sounded just like Ronin. Panting, she looked up into its face. One of its eyes was damaged, a jagged, disc-shaped piece of metal lodged in the socket. The other was a familiar shade of green . “Ronin?” she rasped . “Who else would it be ?” “I thought you were a fucking gearhead!” Her terror subsided, shifting into horror as realization hit her. “Oh my God! What happened to you? Does it hurt ?” She lifted her hands to touch his face, but he turned away before she made contact . “Don’t feel much of anything, right now .” Lara placed her hands on his cheeks. He reached up to brush them away, but stopped when she said, “Don’t.” His metal was warm beneath her palms . “I see the fear in your eyes,” he said. “You don’t have to look at this .” “I thought you were one of them . If I knew it was you…I’d never be afraid of you, Ronin.” Another realization hit her, this one a punch to her gut. “Did I shoot you? Oh , shit !” “You missed.” His lip-plates shifted into what might have been a smile. “We’ll have to talk about that sometime. You’re a terrible shot .” “Not like I ever shot a gun before. God, you look like shit! What happened ?” Carefully, she brushed her thumb along the disc protruding from his eye; it didn’t budge. Her eyes, already stinging, blurred . “A trap.” He settled a hand on her face, wiping a tear from her cheek with a bare-metal finger. “Why are you crying ?” “Look at you.” Her eyes dipped, looking over his ravaged chest, the blackened metal of his arms, the chunks of shrapnel sticking out of him . One metal brow lowered — the other couldn’t move — and he seemed to frown. Gently, he guided her hand away from his face and pushed himself aside. He crawled to the stairs, right leg dragging behind him, and used the railing to pull himself to his feet . Lara scrambled up and hurried to his side. “Let me help .” “No. I’ll manage .” “Dammit, Ronin. Let me at least get that junk out of your leg.” Helplessness left a sour taste in her mouth . “Made it eighteen miles with that junk in my leg. Can make it a little farther .” “Why do you have to be so damned stubborn?” She moved past him and crouched on the steps, putting her at his eye level. “Let me help .” “I’m just being what I am.” Ronin stood upright, casting his scorched arms to either side. “ Look at me .” “What are you talking about ?” “There’s no pretending I ever was, or will be, human. I’m just a reminder of everything you hate .” She stared at him in disbelief. Why was he acting like this? What did she say to set him off, this time? “I don’t hate you. And of course you’re not human. That’s pretty overrated, anyway. You’re Ronin .” “I saw your expression. You can’t stand to look at me, and I can’t blame you for that .” “Is that what you think this is about? Seriously? I was horrified for you !” “What reason would you have to be horrified for me ?” “I know you call it deactivation instead of death, but…you look like you came pretty damn close. You might be tougher than me, but you can still get blown up .” “This damage is largely superficial. I’ve…endured worse.” He dropped his gaze, expression neutral. “This could have been worse. You don’t need to shed tears for me. I’m here. I told you I’d come back, and here I stand .” Her eyes burned anyway. “But you might not have come back,” she said angrily, and lunged at him, wrapping her arms around his torso. He staggered, but she held tight. Ignoring the smell, she focused on his solidness, his heat . * * * W ith most of his skin gone, Ronin’s ability to register touch was limited to raw data, little more than basic pressure readings and estimated temperatures. Despite that, Lara’s embrace was warm and strong. It wasn’t a gesture of fear or uncertainty, but one of relief. She hugged him like she may never have another chance to, like she wasn’t ever going to let him go, and she was right in saying he might never have returned . How could he have been so blind to what he put her through, when he left her like that ? “Doesn’t do us any good to consider what might have been,” he said, slipping his arms around her with great care. Without the more precise sensors in his skin, there was too much chance for error. A slight overapplication of his strength could crush her bones. “This is what happened, and it’s all we’ve got .” “You owe me an apology .” “I’m sorry I left .” “No. For thinking it matters to me what you look like .” Her words swirled through his processors, settling deep in his memory as he contemplated their meaning. His appearance did matter to her, but not in the way he’d thought; she didn’t care if he was a bot or a human, only that he was… himself . She did care about his wellbeing . “Which is like shit right now, correct?” he said, unable to articulate his more complex thoughts . “That’s putting it mildly.” She chuckled . “I’m sorry. Are you going to apologize to me , now ?” “ I have nothing to say sorry for.” When he opened his mouth to reply, she cut him off. “I missed . And you could’ve just called my name and avoided all of that, instead of scaring the shit outta me .” “Shit. Quite a versatile word… I am sorry, Lara Brooks. This was not my intention .” “I know.” She pulled back and looked him over, the humor vanishing from her face. “What can I do to help ?” “Not much. The Clinic is the only place that can repair this damage. You can help get some of the shrapnel out, and remove the damaged skin. At least then we can trade some of it to the scrapper and make a bit of profit from this .” “That’s morbid .” “I am more than the sum of my parts,” he said, and she tilted her head. Another thought chain he could not adequately explain. “It’s practical. If they are removed at the Clinic, they’d likely keep the material to reuse without offering compensation .” He released his embrace and put a hand on the railing, stabilizing himself. With his single optic, he looked up to the second floor. He’d have to handle it just like he had the stairs in that basement; slowly, one step at a time . “I moved my tools into my pack. Bring them upstairs. I’ll meet you there .” “ Okay .” Ronin waited for her to go up first, calculating the journey ahead. His upward climb was slow, but rhythmic — the creak of the bannister, the groan of a step, the thump of his damaged leg. Over and over, until he finally reached the top, one minute and twenty-two seconds later . He kept his head angled down as he walked toward his bedroom, staring at the metal planes of his chest and abdomen, at the charred clothing and melted skin. Ronin bore little resemblance to a human, currently, but she’d thrown her arms around him and showed concern for his state. She wouldn’t have done the same, that first night . They’d both changed so much in so short a time . Lara had the tools spread out on the chest when he entered the room. Some of them were smudged with soot or singed, grips warped from the heat of the flames . “Lie down and tell me what to do .” He moved to the bed, right leg dragging despite his best efforts. The bedding was rumpled, the blankets tossed aside. Had she been sleeping here when came home ? The springs creaked as he sat, and he recalled the many sounds the bed had made during more pleasant activities. He swung his left leg up without trouble. Lara only allowed him a moment’s struggle with the right before she took hold of his ankle and helped him lift it . “Lie back.” She pressed her palms to his chest and guided him down onto the mattress . “You’ll need to pull out the shrapnel. Use the pliers .” Lara went to the chest, selecting a pair of pliers, and returned to the bedside. Catching her lower lip between her teeth, she looked over Ronin. Without a word, she climbed atop him, swinging a leg over his hips to straddle his abdomen, her back facing him. The hem of her shirt slid up her thighs, bare skin warm against his casing . She looked over her shoulder. “So…just pull them out? It won’t hurt you, right? Won’t do any more damage ?” “I’ll be fine,” he said. “Just take out whatever you can get to.” Her heat pulsed through his pants, and her position… He focused his attention on the points of damage. This wasn’t a sexual act; she was simply assisting in the first step toward repairing his casing . Turning away, she muttered to herself. “Okay, I can do this.” She leaned forward and tore his pants open around his knee. There was no pain when she worked the pliers into the hole, only pressure and the sound of metal scraping metal . “In there good.” She shifted atop him, and if he had skin… “Think I got it .” Lara leaned back, and Ronin’s internal sensors registered the force she exerted on the shard in his knee. Electric sparked up his leg . The pliers’ teeth snapped together suddenly, and Lara jerked back. He placed his hands on her sides to steady her, but her torso fell atop his, her hair covering his face. His olfactory sensors detected its fresh, clean scent. He lamented that his bare face plates weren’t sensitive enough to feel the delicate brush of the strands . At least he could feel her weight and the pleasurable warmth she emitted . “I almost got it .” He dropped his hands to her hips and helped her sit up. She pressed the pliers into the hole, but Ronin’s focus moved to her bare legs, her thighs squeezing his abdomen; he was her anchor. Given the situation, it seemed an odd place for his attention . Why was the play of her leg muscles so fascinating, so alluring? Why did her touch mute the concerns that should’ve been the foremost of his processes? He should have gone directly to the Clinic when he arrived in Cheyenne, should have received the necessary repairs before returning to her. Should have spared Lara all this worry . Thoughts of her had carried him back over the uneven landscape. His worry had not been for himself or the damage he’d sustained, but for her. How might she have reacted to Ronin’s deactivation ? It was a foolish thought-chain to pursue; realistically, the chances of her having ever learned of his demise were extremely small. But he wondered, anyway — what would she have done, without him? She’d survived on her own before they met. Ronin knew she would have carried on. Escaping the Bot District would have proven difficult, but Lara was resourceful . Metal groaned as she pulled. Her legs tightened around him. “ Almost …” Ronin exerted gradual pressure opposite hers, pushing his leg down to the bed while she tugged upward. She leaned back, her hair draping over his blackened chest-casing, and released a sound from deep in her throat that was at once a growl and a grunt . With a metallic pang, she fell backward again . “Got the bastard!” She raised the pliers, showing Ronin the jagged, deformed chunk of metal gripped between the teeth . Lara slid off him as he sat up. He drew his right foot toward his rear, slowly bending his knee. Internally, the actuator whined and vibrated, giving diminished return on the power it drew. It was still a significant improvement over the joint being locked in place . “Thank you,” he said, turning his optics to Lara . “We ain’t done yet,” she replied, tucking a mass of wild curls behind her ear. Extending her hand, she touched a finger to one of the pieces of shrapnel protruding from his chest casing. “What happened, anyway? Your upper half is torn to shreds, but your bottom half looks mostly fine .” “Found an old house out there. There were blankets and sheets hung up all over the basement .” She tugged one of the pieces from his chest. His sensors registered a tiny flare of pain, a ghostly echo of what he’d suffered in that basement. It wasn’t nearly as haunting as the corpses . “Keep talking,” she said, moving the pliers to the next bit . “By sheer chance, I missed the tripwire when I worked my way to the back. There was a workbench there. Picked up the reloading press and some cans — ” Lara’s hand jerked back, pulling another piece of shrapnel. She dropped it into his lap . “—and decided it was time to head back,” he continued as she removed more. “I’d already searched the other rooms. As I pushed through the hangings, something caught on my leg. Heard the device arm, but I wasn’t fast enough. The people who lived there rigged up an explosive, probably their final line of defense, and I triggered it .” “I’m glad it wasn’t worse.” She grunted, rocking the pliers to loosen one of the larger pieces . “The sheets caught fire and enveloped me. I tripped over my pack, which is probably what saved me from further damage. It kicked up a cloud of dust that put out the flames.” He glanced down at his arms and brushed the last bits of his ruined coat’s sleeves onto the floor . “All right. Only one left, as far as I can tell.” She settled her eyes on his damaged optic. “Do you…want me to try to get it out ?” “No,” he replied. “The risk of further damage to my optical system is too high .” “What else can I do , then ?” Ronin hesitated. She’d called it morbid, but it was a necessity, and it would earn a little credit in the Market. “Need to get the damaged skin off. I had to disable the entire interface due to shorted sensors .” Her eyes widened, falling to his abdomen, where most of the blackened, melted skin clung .
